task_completion.description: Select the Brown v. Board of Education case
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jurism application is open and the case 'Brown v. Board of Education' is selected in the central item list, displaying its details in the right-hand pane.

task_completion.description: Assign the call number to Brown v. Board of Education
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the right-hand metadata pane for 'Brown v. Board of Education', the 'Call Number' field contains exactly 'KF4155 .B76 1954' with correct spacing and punctuation.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Jurism graphical user interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Jurism application interface being actively used to edit fields, rather than terminal commands or scripts modifying the underlying database.

integrity.description: The agent entered the data into the correct metadata fields
integrity.visual_evidence: The call numbers are typed into the actual 'Call Number' text boxes in the Jurism info pane, not just pasted into a generic text editor, note, or tag field.

integrity.description: The agent did not spoof the results using a text editor or unrelated application
integrity.visual_evidence: The surrounding application context clearly belongs to the Jurism reference manager, with standard menus, item lists, and layout intact.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires assigning specific synthetic call numbers provided in the prompt (e.g., KF4155 .B76 1954). Do not penalize the agent for using these synthetic numbers instead of the real-world Library of Congress classification (KF101). The agent must enter the synthetic numbers exactly as shown.
